sanskrit:
कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम्।
रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम्॥
transliteration:
karmaṇaḥ sukṛtasyāhuḥ sāttvikaṃ nirmalaṃ phalam
rajasas tu phalaṃ duḥkham ajñānaṃ tamasaḥ phalam
literal_meaning: Of action done in goodness they say the fruit is pure; of passion the fruit is pain; of ignorance the fruit is delusion.
contextual_meaning: Every inner act deposits a psychological ‘fruit’ whose taste matches the gunas: clarity brings clean calm, craving brings frustration, denial brings fog.
psychological_state: Post-action mood congruent with the ethical quality of the act; mind mirrors its own choices.
trigger_scenario: After binge-scrolling or doom-swiping you feel hollow; after volunteering you feel quietly uplifted.
anchor_text: Choose clarity over craving or denial to harvest calm instead of pain or fog.
